[Group] [Alarms] Observations

notes.total: 176 tickets in scope represents a healthy pipeline with strong team capacity utilization across Q2.
notes.completed: 175 of 176 tickets completed (99%) demonstrates exceptional execution discipline and quarter goal achievement.
notes.completion_rate: 99% completion rate is outstanding and reflects highly effective sprint planning and delivery.
notes.releases_shipped: 18 releases shipped across 6 sprints (median 2 per sprint) indicates strong continuous delivery cadence with frequent production deployments.
notes.oos_total: Zero out-of-sprint tickets demonstrates excellent sprint planning discipline and no reactive work infiltrating sprints.
notes.oos_open: Zero open OOS items confirms pristine sprint hygiene with no unplanned debt carried forward.
notes.type_split: Healthy work mix with 54% tasks, 26% stories, and 19% bugs — bug ratio is well-controlled and indicates good product quality.
notes.avg_releases: Median 2 releases per sprint (mean 3.0) shows consistent shipping cadence with some sprints delivering larger batches.
notes.time_logged: Only 17 hours logged against 2082 estimated hours (1% tracking) indicates severe time-tracking breakdown — data unreliable for capacity planning.
notes.estimate_accuracy: Cannot assess estimate accuracy with only 1% of time logged — time tracking must be enforced for future quarters.
notes.no_estimate: 18% of tickets (32 items) lacked estimates, slightly above ideal but within acceptable range for planning reliability.
notes.rollover: Single rollover ticket (1%) across the quarter is excellent and shows strong sprint commitment discipline.
notes.cycle_time: Median 12-day cycle time is reasonable for scope, though mean 17.3 days suggests some tickets experienced delays — investigate outliers.
notes.assignee_workload: Riccardo Fachin carried 24% of the load (43 tickets), significantly above team average — consider rebalancing to prevent bottlenecks and burnout risk.
